theme: Tune a four-bar linkage to trace a path
title: Four-Bar Coupler Tracing: Fit a Linkage to a Target Path
strategies: baseline: emit a fixed un-tuned Grashof crank-rocker sized from the ground span (reproduces the checker baseline, ~0.1)

greedy recipe: blind local optimization of all five linkage parameters (a,b,c,u,v) from random restarts, minimizing the point-set distance -- wanders into shallow local minima and barely-rotating linkages

strong insight: read the radial invariants -- every target point lies at distance in [a-rho, a+rho] from pivot O0 and [c-rho2, c+rho2] from O1 -- to recover a, rho=|P-A|, c, rho2=|P-B| algebraically, collapsing the 5-D search to a 1-D sweep over the coupler length b (u,v then pinned by law of cosines), then a light polish
full local-search refinement of the strong seed on both assembly branches
